subject: Databases
title: The Birth of SQL
description: A micro-lesson that makes The Birth of SQL usable.

hook.text: In the 1970s, IBM researchers needed a language to talk to relational databases. SQL was born.

buildup.text: Raymond Boyce and Donald Chamberlin created SEQUEL, later renamed SQL, based on Codd's relational model.

discovery.text: SQL reads like English: SELECT name FROM users WHERE age > 18. Even non-programmers can follow it.

twist.text: Despite being 50+ years old, SQL outlived thousands of newer query languages. Nothing replaced it.

climax.text: Today SQL is the most widely used database language on the planet.

punchline.text: SQL: born in the 70s, still running the world.

quiz.question: Why has SQL endured for over 50 years?
options: It reads like English and nothing has successfully replaced it for querying relational data
It is the fastest programming language ever created
It was designed for AI applications
